clinical_history: 21 year-old female seen in the emergency department with complaints of chest pain and shortness of breath. What does this ECG show?
diagnosis: This ECG is entirely within the normal limits.  The rhythm is sinus at about 78 bpm. Basic intervals are as follows: QRS duration 0.08s; PR interval 0.13s; and QT/QTc interval 0.36s/0.39s--all are within normal limits. The QRS axis is also normal at about +70 degrees. There is no ECG evidence of left or right ventricular hypertrophy. P wave morphology and duration are normal. R wave progression is normal, as are the ST-T waves.
